obligations.rule: The validator must correctly identify valid and invalid email addresses based on the default options.
obligations.why: Baseline functionality as described in the README.

obligations.rule: If `allow_display_name` is true, the validator must accept emails in the format `Display Name <email-address>`.
obligations.why: Option-sensitive behavior described in the README.

obligations.rule: If `require_display_name` is true, the validator must reject emails without a display name.
obligations.why: Option-sensitive behavior described in the README.

obligations.rule: If `allow_utf8_local_part` is false, the validator must reject emails with non-English UTF8 characters in the local part.
obligations.why: Option-sensitive behavior described in the README.

obligations.rule: If `require_tld` is false, the validator must accept emails without a TLD in the domain part.
obligations.why: Option-sensitive behavior described in the README.

obligations.rule: If `allow_ip_domain` is true, the validator must accept emails with IP addresses in the domain part.
obligations.why: Option-sensitive behavior described in the README.

obligations.rule: If `blacklisted_chars` is set, the validator must reject emails containing any of the specified characters in the local part.
obligations.why: Option-sensitive behavior described in the README.

obligations.rule: If `host_blacklist` is set, the validator must reject emails with domains matching any entry in the blacklist.
obligations.why: Option-sensitive behavior described in the README.

obligations.rule: If `host_whitelist` is set, the validator must reject emails with domains not matching any entry in the whitelist.
obligations.why: Option-sensitive behavior described in the README.

obligations.rule: Ensure early return behavior is triggered for invalid display names when `require_display_name` or `allow_display_name` is true.
obligations.why: Validate the branch handling display name validation.

obligations.rule: Ensure early return behavior is triggered for emails exceeding the default maximum length.
obligations.why: Validate the branch handling maximum length checks.

obligations.rule: Cover uncovered statement at line 49: if (display_name_without_quotes === display_name) { return false; }
obligations.why: Ensure validation rejects display names that are identical to their unquoted version.

obligations.rule: Cover uncovered branch at line 110: options.domain_specific_validation && (lower_domain === 'gmail.com' || lower_domain === 'googlemail.com')
obligations.why: Ensure domain-specific validation correctly handles Gmail and Googlemail domains.

obligations.rule: Cover uncovered statement at line 124: if (!isByteLength(username.replace(/\./g, ''), { min: 6, max: 30 })) { return false; }
obligations.why: Ensure validation rejects Gmail usernames that do not meet length constraints after removing dots.

obligations.rule: Cover uncovered statement at line 41: return false;
obligations.why: Ensure the validator rejects empty or whitespace-only display names.

obligations.rule: Cover uncovered branch at line 49: display_name_without_quotes === display_name (both true and false branches).
obligations.why: Ensure the validator handles cases where the display name is identical to its unquoted version.

obligations.rule: Cover uncovered branch at line 56: !all_start_with_back_slash (both true and false branches).
obligations.why: Ensure the validator correctly handles display names with improperly escaped quotes.

obligations.rule: Cover uncovered statement at line 50: return false;
obligations.why: Tests rejection of display names identical to their unquoted version.

obligations.rule: Cover uncovered statement at line 57: return false;
obligations.why: Tests rejection of improperly escaped quotes in display names.

obligations.rule: Cover uncovered ternary paths at lines 172-173: quotedEmailUserUtf8.test(user) and quotedEmailUser.test(user).
obligations.why: Tests validation of quoted local parts with and without UTF-8 support.

obligations.rule: Uncovered statement at line 93: return false.
obligations.why: Targets uncovered statement at line 93 by testing rejection of emails exceeding the maximum length when ignore_max_length is false.

obligations.rule: Uncovered if path `condition_true_branch` at line 130; condition: !gmailUserPart.test(user_parts[i]).
obligations.why: Targets uncovered branch at line 130 by testing Gmail username validation with domain-specific rules.

obligations.rule: Uncovered if path `condition_true_branch` at line 153; condition: !domain.startsWith('[') || !domain.endsWith(']').
obligations.why: Targets uncovered branch at line 153 by testing validation of IP domains with incorrect brackets.

test_groups.title: Default email validation
test_groups.rationale: Covers baseline email validation with default options.

test_groups.title: Validation with allow_display_name=true
test_groups.rationale: Tests the behavior when display names are allowed.

test_groups.title: Validation with require_display_name=true
test_groups.rationale: Ensures emails without display names are rejected when required.

test_groups.title: Validation with allow_utf8_local_part=false
test_groups.rationale: Ensures non-English UTF8 characters in the local part are rejected.

test_groups.title: Validation with require_tld=false
test_groups.rationale: Tests behavior when TLD is not required.

test_groups.title: Validation with allow_ip_domain=true
test_groups.rationale: Ensures IP addresses in the domain part are accepted when allowed.

test_groups.title: Validation with blacklisted_chars set
test_groups.rationale: Tests rejection of emails containing blacklisted characters in the local part.

test_groups.title: Validation with host_blacklist set
test_groups.rationale: Ensures domains in the blacklist are rejected.

test_groups.title: Validation with host_whitelist set
test_groups.rationale: Ensures only whitelisted domains are accepted.

test_groups.title: Validation for maximum email length
test_groups.rationale: Tests rejection of emails exceeding the maximum length.

test_groups.title: Validation for display names identical to unquoted version
test_groups.rationale: Tests rejection of display names that are identical to their unquoted version.
